caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Supplied 4-digit 5416 mix is not a 541612 occupation census.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The protected credential/data segment may be small or large by firm.
inputs.pi_dist: Actual retainer, project and subscription mix is unknown.
inputs.pi_moat: Credentialed human requirements support terminal demand and are not counted again as a capture uplift.
inputs.t50_years: SHRM surveys HR professionals, not NAICS 541612 firms.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Population is HR functions broadly, not small US HR consultancies.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No evidentiary basis; low-confidence placeholder.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Founder retirement in an acquired UK firm is not sector-wide shortage evidence.
inputs.active_consolidators: Mixed brokerage, wealth and UK activities limit NAICS specificity.
inputs.active_consolidators: S7 confirms an in-scope US advisory buyout but does not disclose price or earnings.
inputs.buy_mult: No disclosed US HR-advisory transaction price and EBITDA were found in the reviewed source set.
inputs.buy_mult: S8 is a public, much larger workforce-development comparable, not a $1-10M EBITDA fee-only target.
inputs.buy_mult: An SDE-to-EBITDA conversion is not defensible without target owner compensation and QoE adjustments.
inputs.exit_mult: The equality is a conservative re-estimation, not evidence of a 6.2x small-boutique clearing price.
inputs.exit_mult: The matched US transaction establishes activity only; no financial terms are disclosed.
inputs.pricing_structure: No industry-wide revenue-mix survey was found.

inputs.active_consolidators: The cited set does not cleanly establish three independent US fee-only HR-consulting platforms; Aon and NFP are related, S5 is UK, and S7's current page does not expose the named Inspire HR row.
inputs.historical_analogs: S1 and S2 support current AI adoption and task redesign, not the claimed historical progression of HRIS and survey platforms or regulated review.

inputs.pi_moat / cross_checks.terminal_value: The pi_moat rationale invokes credentials despite the record's statement that credentialed human requirements are used only for terminal demand, creating a non-thesis-changing capture/terminal overlap.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $173,623 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541612); class <5: 5,881 firms, avg revenue/firm $394,584 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $61,752 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 651 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,244,538 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $194,770 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 412 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,382,619 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $372,880 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 431 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,933,174 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,241,542 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 144 firms, avg revenue/firm $29,228,493 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,574,259 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 122 firms, avg revenue/firm $121,304,852 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $18,984,209 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 575

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Routine analysis, drafting, survey operations and client admin are AI-exposed; executive advisory and credentialed sign-off persist. Residual corrects a parent-level mix that omits HR-consulting specialties.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541612 ($10,678,955,284) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541612 ($26,539,675,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: All deterministic mechanics, exact-artifact identity, dataset equality, arithmetic and byte-identical rerender checks pass. Reopened evidence supports the 39% adoption anchor, task-direction thesis, ERISA credential floor and the disclosed 6.2x public EBITDA reference. The record remains publishable with its authored proxy and uncertainty caveats plus non-fatal caveats on the exact consolidator count, historical-analogue support and credential-related capture/terminal overlap; none changes the M=0, S=0 kill conclusion.

sources.title: SHRM State of AI in HR 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Survey of 1,908 HR professionals: 39% reported AI adopted in HR functions, with use concentrated in particular HR practice areas.

sources.title: WorldatWork: AI impacting rewards, skills and compensation
sources.what_it_supports: 2026 total-rewards survey commentary: routine work is automated and roles redesigned, while current impact remains early to moderate.

sources.title: IRS Enrolled Actuary Information
sources.what_it_supports: Describes enrolled actuaries approved to perform actuarial services required under ERISA.

sources.title: Aon completes acquisition of NFP
sources.what_it_supports: Documents active middle-market acquisition across brokerage, benefits consulting, wealth and retirement advisory, and its mixed-business scope.

sources.title: NFP acquires HELPGB
sources.what_it_supports: Documents acquisition of an HR and health-and-safety consultancy and founder retirement; UK scope is stated.

sources.title: BizBuySell Service Business Valuation Multiples
sources.what_it_supports: National 2021-2025 sold service-business SDE earnings-multiple distribution; broad proxy rather than HR-consulting comp.

sources.title: PrivSource Professional Services Buyout Acquisitions in 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Reports HR Path's March 2026 buyout of US-based HR advisory firm Inspire Human Resources; financial terms are not disclosed.

sources.title: Navagant Workforce Development Market Update, May 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Reports April 2026 TEV/LTM EBITDA data including 6.2x for Korn Ferry and separately lists a recent HR-consultancy transaction; public-company scale and business mix differ from a small private HR consultancy.

title: Human Resources Consulting Services
